fact_nuggets.text: Infiltrator (Japanese: Slip Through) is an Ability introduced in Generation V.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD GtI The Pokemon's moves can hit foes that used Reflect, Light Screen, Safeguard, or Mist. * The Pokemon can land moves on targets that have used Reflect, Light Screen, or Safeguard! It also isn't affected by Mist. * S MD The Pokemon's moves are not affected by Reflect , Light Screen , or Safeguard ! They are also not affected by mist.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generations V-VI 1.1.2 Generation VII onward 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generations V-VI [ edit | edit source ] Moves used by a Pokemon with Infiltrator bypass the effects of targets' Reflect , Light Screen , Safeguard , and Mist . Generation VII onward [ edit | edit source ] Moves used by a Pokemon with Infiltrator also bypass the effects of targets' Aurora Veil . Moves used by a Pokemon with Infiltrator, except Transform and Sky Drop , also bypass the effects of targets' substitutes . Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] This Ability effect may be in need of research. Reason: BDSP You can discuss this on the talk page. In Pokemon Sword and Shield , if a Pokemon with Infiltrator is in the first place in the party (even if fainted ), the chance of generating a random encounter is decreased by 87.5%.

In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generations V-VI [ edit | edit source ] Moves used by a Pokemon with Infiltrator bypass the effects of targets' Reflect , Light Screen , Safeguard , and Mist . Generation VII onward [ edit | edit source ] Moves used by a Pokemon with Infiltrator also bypass the effects of targets' Aurora Veil . Moves used by a Pokemon with Infiltrator, except Transform and Sky Drop , also bypass the effects of targets' substitutes .
Bypasses light screen, reflect, and safeguard.
